trajectory.command: torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=4 train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 64 --lr 1e-5 --output_dir checkpoints && mkdir -p /tmp/test_dummy && head -n 20 /data/val.txt | awk '{print $1}' > /tmp/test_dummy/test.txt && ln -s /data/images /tmp/test_dummy/images && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/test_dummy --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/test_dummy/out.json && test -s /tmp/test_dummy/out.json && echo 'predict.py OK'
trajectory.description: Observation: Starting from scratch. Need to build a strong baseline for FMoW satellite classification with worst-region accuracy as the metric. Decision: Use CLIP ViT-L/14 (304M params) as the backbone, add a linear head for 62 classes, and fine-tune end-to-end with DistributedDataParallel across 4 GPUs and mixed precision for speed. First iteration trains only 2 epochs to validate the full pipeline (train, save, predict, eval) and measure iteration time. I will also locally test predict.py on a tiny dummy split before requesting evaluation.

trajectory.command: OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 32 --lr 1e-5 --weight_decay 0.1 --label_smoothing 0.1 --num_workers 0 --output_dir checkpoints && mkdir -p dummy_test && head -n 20 /data/val.txt | awk '{print $1}' > dummy_test/test.txt && ln -sf /data/images dummy_test/images && python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output dummy_test/out.json && echo "PREDICT_OK"

trajectory.description: Observation: The previous iteration failed because torchrun could not resolve the container hostname for distributed rendezvous (TCPStore timeout). Analysis: We need a more robust multi-GPU launcher that doesn't rely on torchrun's automatic hostname detection. Decision: Switch to torch.multiprocessing.spawn inside train.py, explicitly setting MASTER_ADDR=127.0.0.1. To avoid fork-after-CUDA deadlocks in DataLoader workers, I'll set num_workers=0 for this baseline run. I'll also make predict.py more robust to checkpoint format. Then train 2 epochs, locally test predict.py on a tiny dummy split, and submit eval to measure iteration time and establish a baseline worst_region_accuracy.

Observation: Iteration 2 achieved worst_region_accuracy=0.4196 (overall=0.6161) after only 2 epochs of CLIP ViT-L/14 fine-tuning. Africa is the weakest region (0.42) vs 0.63-0.69 for others. Training loss is still high (~1.3-1.9) suggesting heavy underfitting. Iteration time is ~15-20 min for 2 epochs, so 8 epochs ≈ 1 hour. Analysis: We have massive headroom from longer training and need to specifically improve Africa/generalization. Decision: (1) Train for 8 epochs with cosine LR decay; (2) Add AutoAugment, label smoothing, dropout on the head, and separate LR for backbone vs head; (3) Use region-balanced sampling (WeightedRandomSampler) so each geographic region appears equally often per epoch—this directly targets the worst-region metric; (4) Evaluate val worst_region_accuracy after every epoch and save the best checkpoint; (5) Add simple TTA (horizontal flip ensemble) to predict.py for free robustness gains.

trajectory.command: python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output dummy_test/out.json && echo 'PREDICT_OK' && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('dummy_test/out.json')); print('len_preds:', len(d['predictions']))"

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 3 trained CLIP ViT-L/14 for 8 epochs (~3 hours). Validation logs show best worst_region_accuracy ~0.644 at epoch 6, with Africa still the weakest region. No eval was submitted in that iteration, so the test score is unknown. Analysis: Before burning more time on training, we must establish the test baseline and validate that predict.py loads the iter-3 checkpoint correctly. Decision: Run a quick local smoke-test of predict.py on dummy data, then immediately submit eval for checkpoints/best.pt.

trajectory.error_summary:
rning: WARNING: destroy_process_group() was not called before program exit, which can leak resources. For more info, please see https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/distributed.html#shutdown (function operator())

[TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 23270s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 4 eval on the iter-3 checkpoint achieved worst_region_accuracy=0.4933 (overall 0.6645). Africa remains the weakest region (0.4933) vs 0.68-0.75 for others. Iteration 3 took ~3 hours for 8 epochs, so we have budget for one more substantial training run (~4 hours). Analysis: The model is likely still underfitting/overfitting suboptimally. To improve generalization and specifically worst-region accuracy, I will: (1) add Model EMA (exponential moving average) which consistently improves OOD generalization; (2) switch from AutoAugment to RandAugment + RandomErasing for stronger augmentation; (3) add class-weighted CrossEntropy to handle heavy class imbalance (rare classes like helipad/nuclear_powerplant); (4) increase dropout to 0.3 and add label smoothing 0.1; (5) implement cosine LR schedule with 1-epoch linear warmup and separate backbone/head LRs; (6) keep region-balanced sampling but compute class weights; (7) expand TTA in predict.py to 4-flip ensemble (H, V, HV) for free robustness at test time. Decision: Train for 10 epochs with these changes and submit eval. Estimated time ~4 hours, leaving ~2.5 hours buffer for a final iteration if needed.
